title: triticum biflorum var. hornemannii
intro: Triticum biflorum var. hornemannii is an old-world wild wheat species, also known as Hornemann's wheat or Hornemann's wild wheat, which is native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Triticum biflorum var. hornemannii include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
